title: Account Model
content:
- ### OntologyBlock
- ## About Account Model
- ### Project Aurora: AI for Weather Forecasting
  - Project Aurora is a foundation model for weather forecasting, 5,000 times faster than traditional physics-based methods.
  - It is another example of the fifth paradigm, using simulations to train a deep learning emulator.
  - Unlike other emulators, Aurora is trained on diverse data to model fundamental fluid flow equations.
  - It can be fine-tuned for downstream applications with less data, such as pollution flow modelling.
  - [Introducing Aurora: The First Large-Scale Foundation Model of the Atmosphere](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/introducing-aurora-the-first-large-scale-foundation-model-of-the-atmosphere/?msockid=35739e94ab6c69d41b738b93aa076831)
  - [From Forecasting Storms to Designing Molecules: How New AI Foundation Models Can Speed Up Scientific Discovery](https://news.microsoft.com/source/features/ai/from-forecasting-storms-to-designing-molecules-how-new-ai-foundation-models-can-speed-up-scientific-discovery/?msockid=35739e94ab6c69d41b738b93aa076831)
- ## Immersive AR and VR Experiences
  - **Vectorworks Nomad** ([app](https://apps.apple.com/ch/app/vectorworks-nomad/id506706850?l=en-GB))
  - Markerless AR viewer on iOS/Android; place your model at 1 : 1 scale on site without coding.
  - **Desktop VR via Enscape or Twinmotion**
  - One-click VR export to headsets (Oculus, Vive) for real-time walkthroughs.
  - **Custom Game-Engine Solutions**
  - Import via Datasmith into Unreal Engine or FBX/OBJ into Unity for bespoke interactive apps. AI can assist by auto-generating environment assets or textures.
  - **Web-Based AR/VR Viewers**
  - Upload glTF/GLB to platforms like Sketchfab for browser-based AR (USDZ for iOS Quick Look) or VR demos.
- ### Project Aurora: AI for Weather Forecasting
  - Project Aurora is a foundation model for weather forecasting, 5,000 times faster than traditional physics-based methods.
  - It is another example of the fifth paradigm, using simulations to train a deep learning emulator.
  - Unlike other emulators, Aurora is trained on diverse data to model fundamental fluid flow equations.
  - It can be fine-tuned for downstream applications with less data, such as pollution flow modelling.
  - [Introducing Aurora: The First Large-Scale Foundation Model of the Atmosphere](https://www.microsoft.com/en-us/research/blog/introducing-aurora-the-first-large-scale-foundation-model-of-the-atmosphere/?msockid=35739e94ab6c69d41b738b93aa076831)

- ### **Motion Model for Image Animation**
  - [Thin Plate Spline Motion Model](https://replicate.com/yoyo-nb/thin-plate-spline-motion-model) - *   This model animates a still image by warping it according to the motion of a driving video.
- It uses a thin-plate spline motion model to learn [[modeling]] patterns from the driving video.
- The system uses keypoint detection to identify facial landmarks or other features in both the source image and the driving video.
- The thin-plate spline transformation warps the source image so that its keypoints move in accordance with the motion depicted in the driving video.
- Users can input a static image and a video to generate an animated version of the image following the driving video's movements.
- The process involves feature extraction, motion estimation, and image rendering to create the final animated output.
- The tool promotes better understanding of software development processes and architecture decisions.
- Customisation of colours and metrics is possible, enabling the tool to adapt to different codebases and analysis goals.

- The display technology is rapidly evolving, with new advancements in resolution, refresh rates, brightness, and tracking accuracy. As the technology matures, there will be more opportunities to enhance the personalized experiences. - #### 4.12.5 AI and Ideology
- #### 4.12.5 AI and Ideology
- #### 4.12.5 AI and Ideology
- Brief contextual overview
  - The account model in banking and finance refers to the conceptual and technical framework used to represent, manage, and record financial transactions and balances.
  - Modern account models have evolved from simple double-entry ledgers to complex, real-time, and programmatically verified systems, reflecting advances in technology and regulatory requirements.
  - The shift toward digital and API-driven banking has prompted academic interest in the reliability, scalability, and compliance of contemporary account models.
- Key developments and current state
  - The integration of real-time accounting and open banking has led to more dynamic and transparent financial record-keeping.
  - Academic research now focuses on the implications of these models for auditability, fraud prevention, and regulatory compliance.
- Academic foundations
  - The foundational work of Luca Pacioli on double-entry bookkeeping remains relevant, but contemporary models incorporate elements of distributed ledger technology, real-time validation, and standardised data formats.
- Industry adoption and implementations
  - Real-time accounting models are increasingly adopted by fintechs and neobanks, enabling immediate transaction verification and enhanced audit trails.
  - Traditional banks are integrating these models to remain competitive, particularly in response to open banking regulations.
  - Notable organisations and platforms
    - Monzo, Revolut, and Starling Bank in the UK have implemented real-time accounting and open banking APIs.
    - UK Finance and the Open Banking Implementation Entity (OBIE) have played key roles in standardising account models for interoperability.
  - UK and North England examples where relevant
    - In Manchester, fintechs such as Zopa and Atom Bank have leveraged real-time accounting to streamline lending and savings products.
    - Leeds-based banks have adopted open banking to enhance customer data access and service personalisation.
    - Newcastle and Sheffield have seen growth in digital banking hubs, with local credit unions and challenger banks adopting modern account models.
- Technical capabilities and limitations
  - Real-time accounting models allow for immediate transaction validation, reducing the risk of errors and fraud.
  - These models support complex transaction splits and merges, enabling precise tracking of funds and fees.
  - Limitations include the need for robust cybersecurity measures and the challenge of integrating legacy systems.
- Standards and frameworks
  - The Financial Data Exchange (FDX) and Open Banking UK standards provide a framework for secure and standardised data sharing.
  - The Bank Accounting Advisory Series (BAAS) from the Office of the Chief Accountant (OCA) offers guidance on best practices for account model implementation.
- Key academic papers and sources
  - Pacioli, L. (1494). *Summa de arithmetica, geometria, proportioni et proportionalita*. Venice: Paganino Paganini. (Original work on double-entry bookkeeping)
  - Christidis, K., & Devetsikiotis, M. (2016). "Blockchains and Smart Contracts for the Internet of Things." *IEEE Access*, 4, 2292–2303. https://doi.org/10.1109/ACCESS.2016.2566339
  - Tapscott, D., & Tapscott, A. (2016). *Blockchain Revolution: How the Technology Behind Bitcoin Is Changing Money, Business, and the World*. Penguin. https://www.penguinrandomhouse.com/books/230966/blockchain-revolution-by-don-tapscott-and-alex-tapscott/
  - Open Banking Implementation Entity (OBIE). (2025). *Open Banking Standards*. https://www.openbanking.org.uk/standards
  - Office of the Chief Accountant (OCA). (2025). *Bank Accounting Advisory Series (BAAS)*. https://www.occ.treas.gov/publications-and-resources/publications/banker-education/files/pub-bank-accounting-advisory-series.pdf
- Ongoing research directions
  - Research is exploring the integration of generative AI and machine learning in account models to enhance predictive analytics and fraud detection.
  - Studies are also investigating the impact of real-time accounting on regulatory compliance and audit efficiency.
- British contributions and implementations
  - The UK has been a leader in open banking, with the OBIE setting global standards for secure data sharing.
  - British fintechs have pioneered the use of real-time accounting models, particularly in the lending and savings sectors.
- North England innovation hubs (if relevant)
  - Manchester, Leeds, Newcastle, and Sheffield have become centres for fintech innovation, with local universities and businesses collaborating on advanced account models.
  - The Northern Powerhouse initiative has supported the growth of digital banking hubs in these cities.
- Regional case studies
  - Zopa Bank in Manchester has implemented a real-time accounting model to streamline its lending processes and improve customer transparency.
  - Leeds Building Society has adopted open banking to enhance its digital services and customer engagement.
- Emerging trends and developments
  - The integration of generative AI and machine learning is expected to further enhance the capabilities of account models.
  - The rise of decentralised finance (DeFi) and blockchain-based account models may challenge traditional banking frameworks.
- Anticipated challenges
  - Ensuring the security and privacy of financial data in real-time and open banking environments remains a significant challenge.
  - Regulatory compliance and interoperability between different account models will require ongoing attention.
- Research priorities
  - Research should focus on the long-term impact of real-time and open banking on financial stability and consumer protection.
  - Studies should also explore the potential of blockchain and DeFi in transforming account models.

- **Regional Context**: UK/North England where applicable

ontology.definition: Balance-based accounting model within blockchain systems, providing essential functionality for distributed ledger technology operations and properties.
